answer:
A Professor Emeritus receives a consolidated honorarium of Rs. 200,000 per month (all-inclusive), which includes an annual increase of 5% (Source: Framework-PE.pdf).

A Distinguished National Professor (DNP) awardee receives an honorarium of Rs. 200,000 on a bi-annual basis (Source: Framework-DNP.pdf).

answer:
Regarding the appointment of Vice Chancellors, the provided documents contain the following information:

*   **Professor Emeritus (PE):** In cases where Vice Chancellors join as PE at newly developed universities (other than their parent university), the HEC may invite three preferences from newly developed universities, or the Vice Chancellors may offer their services as Professor Emeritus themselves. [Source: Framework-PE.pdf]
*   **BPS-22 Appointment:** A Professor appointed as Vice-Chancellor in BPS-22 shall be allowed a personal grade of BPS-22 as Professor after relinquishing the office of Vice-Chancellor, provided they have completed one tenure as Vice-Chancellor and were a regular Professor in a Public Sector University prior to their appointment. This personal grade does not count toward the 12.5% quota of BPS-22. [Source: Framework-MP.pdf]

I cannot provide information regarding "all four HEC professor appointment frameworks" as only two documents were provided.
